label: About
title: About Nocturne Aperture Section
short_blurb: A daily generated front page where the image is the interface.
body: Daily Frontpage is a daily generated interactive front page. Each edition starts from recent saved signals and source research, becomes a new image-led scene, and stays explorable in the archive. The image is the interface: visible marks, objects, and surfaces in the scene open the real sources that shaped the day.

This iteration began with 79 recent saved-signal files and 66 captured links, then narrowed the page to 9 source windows around @compositioning: Drawings by Jans Muskee (2014-2025) and @ttiimmees: ok so the problem wasn't the algorithm, plus 7 more. A source image from jansmuskee.nl helped set the palette, contrast, edge behavior, and gesture. The scene became Nocturne Aperture Section: Nocturnal painted observatory cutaway, architectural section camera grammar: a tall ochre viewing aperture glows behind smoky gray plaster, cropped by a heavy left jamb and a dark central slab. Translate the reference into shadow mass, glass edge, low foreground block, scuffed wall fields, and off-axis rail breaks. After the image was made, a plate-reading pass mapped 9 visible regions and the mask audit tightened them against left ochre glowing aperture, central black-brown vertical slab, and cyan-dark side pane in left recess.

typography.rationale: Bricolage Grotesque and Space Grotesk fit record, arcade, and listening-room editions while Fraunces keeps source captions tactile.
